response: The global food system faces mounting pressure as the population is expected to hit 9.7 billion by 2050, demanding a 60% increase in food production amid climate change, water scarcity, and degraded soils—33% of arable land has lost productivity. Agriculture itself drives about 25% of greenhouse gas emissions, while roughly one-third of all food produced is wasted. Proposed solutions include precision agriculture, vertical farming, alternative proteins, drought-resistant GMOs, regenerative practices, and policy reforms on subsidies and carbon pricing. The central challenge is scaling these innovations while keeping food affordable for the world's poorest populations.
